name: cbor2 (CBOR Binary Serialization for Python)

performance.notes: C extension available; significantly faster than pure Python; competitive with msgpack

agent_readiness.notes: CBOR (RFC 7049/8949) binary serialization — the JSON of binary formats. import cbor2. data = cbor2.dumps({'key': 'value', 'nums': [1, 2, 3]}). obj = cbor2.loads(data). File: cbor2.dump(obj, fp); cbor2.load(fp). CBOR tags for typed data: CBORTag(100, value). Datetime, UUID, Decimal, bytes natively supported. Canonical encoding for deterministic output. Streaming decode. C extension: optional but fast. CBOR used in COSE, CTAP2, IoT protocols, FIDO2.

gotchas: Less ecosystem tooling than JSON or msgpack
CBOR tags can be confusing for new users
C extension is optional — install with pip install cbor2[c] for performance
best_when: IoT and embedded systems using CBOR protocol (CoAP, COSE, CTAP2)
When structured binary serialization with datetime/UUID/Decimal native types is needed
Replacing JSON with compact binary format in size-constrained environments
avoid_when: High-performance messaging where msgpack has more ecosystem support
summary: cbor2 implements CBOR (RFC 8949) binary serialization — cbor2.dumps(obj) / cbor2.loads(data). JSON-like API but compact binary output. Native support for datetime, UUID, Decimal, bytes. Streaming decode, canonical encoding. C extension for performance. MIT by Alex Grönholm. Used in FIDO2/WebAuthn, COSE, CoAP, CTAP2 protocols. Best when CBOR is required by protocol (IoT/security); for general binary serialization use msgpack.
